fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Minnesota Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Minnesota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity minnesota, how to transfer occupational therapist license to minnesota, occupational therapist compact minnesota, minnesota occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Minnesota Board of Medical Practice — OT
board.mailing_address:
Minnesota Board of Occupational Therapy
P.O. Box 64972
St. Paul, MN 55164-0972

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) exam

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Minnesota.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Access the MN Online Licensing System, select 'Register a New Account,' and then complete and submit the initial application forms, paying the $150 application fee.

steps.title: Arrange for Transcript Submission
steps.description: Request your official transcripts, verifying graduation from an accredited occupational therapy program, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Minnesota Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Submit NBCOT Score Report
steps.description: Have your official NBCOT certification examination score report sent directly from NBCOT to the Minnesota Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: After submitting your online application, follow the instructions on the Office of Justice Programs website (https://www.ojcc.state.mn.us/criminal-background-checks) to complete the required CBC process.

steps.title: Pay Initial License Fee
steps.description: Upon approval of your application, log back into the MN Online Licensing System to pay the $100 initial license fee.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Minnesota offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Minnesota Board of Medical Practice — OT for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Minnesota is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Minnesota without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Minnesota also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

faqs.question: How much does a Minnesota OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $150. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Minnesota have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Minnesota has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Minnesota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Minnesota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Minnesota is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Minnesota without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Minnesota also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.
